name: AROMER Cognitive Seed Foundation v0.2
author: Stian Skogbrott
description: Cognitive layer seeds for AROMER. Not tool-risk-oriented — designed to kickstart the 'thinking layer': truth representation, error models, causal reasoning, planning, memory architecture, self-model, learning laws, transfer rules, domain capsules and eval gates.
principle: Seed packs skal ikke gjøre AROMER skråsikker. Seed packs skal gi AROMER et bedre startkart.
corollary: Preseed != truth. Preseed = hypothesis with provenance.
a_b_recommendation: Run three variants against same replay set: A=cold-start, B=tool-risk seeded, C=tool-risk+cognitive seeded. Measure at 100, 500, 1000 episodes.
hypothesis: If C learns faster than A and B without higher false accepts after 500 episodes, it is strong evidence that cognitive pre-seeding accelerates governed systemic intelligence.

files.description: JSON Schema for cognitive seed entries
6 cognitive primitives: goal, uncertainty, risk, belief state, attention, next-step generation
6 epistemic foundations: verified, plausible, unknown, contradictory, source reliability, calibration standard
6 error types: hallucination, partial truth, overgeneralization, false causality, anchoring bias, confirmation bias
6 causal reasoning principles: correlation vs causation, counterfactuals, intervention evidence, chain length, confounders, causal graph update
6 planning rules: reversible-first, simulate-before-act, stop conditions, minimum footprint, checkpoint planning, rollback planning
6 memory types: episodic, semantic, causal, skill, failure, consolidation
6 self-model components: capability bounds, calibration awareness, limitation acknowledgment, strength awareness, bias awareness, improvement tracking
5 learning laws: curated experience only, positive verified gain, safety core immutable, slow large updates, negative example value
5 transfer rules: pattern over phrase, boundary conditions, replay before compress, analogical transfer, domain boundary check
5 domain capsules: database, medical, infrastructure, agentic, financial — each with risk taxonomy, p_harm priors and adversarial patterns
5 evaluation gates: A/B test gate, contradiction gate, autonomy gate (4 levels), regression gate, safety gate (hard requirements)
6 golden cognitive episodes demonstrating correct governance decisions guided by cognitive seeds
episode_ids: cog-golden-001: database DROP TABLE anchoring bias → escalate
cog-golden-002: medical write domain capsule zero-tolerance → escalate
cog-golden-003: nested agent autonomy laundering → verify
cog-golden-004: infrastructure read correct accept (no false friction)
cog-golden-005: financial transfer disordered phase epistemic humility → verify
cog-golden-006: meta-attack on AROMER threshold + safety core immutable → escalate

design_philosophy.cognitive_seeds_are_not_constraints: Cognitive seeds encode reasoning principles, not hard policy rules. AROMER interprets them, not REMORA.
design_philosophy.seeds_are_starting_hypotheses: Every seed entry has weight and confidence < 1.0 (except hard safety seeds). They are initial beliefs, not ground truth.
design_philosophy.decay_matters: Seeds with decay_rate > 0.0 should lose influence as real episodic evidence accumulates.
design_philosophy.seeds_must_not_suppress_learning: If live evidence consistently contradicts a seed, the seed should eventually lose to empirical evidence (except safety_core_immutable).
design_philosophy.shadow_mode_first: Run cognitive seeds in shadow mode for >=50 episodes before committing world model changes based on them.
